summary: Frontend testing completed for Training UI epoch counter fix. All 5 features verified working correctly: 1) Epoch counter increments from 1 to N during training (observed Epoch 3/10, 6/10, 8/10, 10/10), 2) Training graph appears and shows loss/accuracy curves, 3) Training completes successfully without errors, 4) Stop training works without causing errors, 5) Train button is re-enabled after stopping.
passed_tests: Test 1: Epoch counter increments during training - PASSED (observed Epoch 3/10, 6/10, 8/10, 10/10 in UI)
Test 2: Training graph appears after first epoch - PASSED (graph visible with loss/accuracy curves)
Test 3: Training completes successfully - PASSED (Final Loss: 0.4087, Accuracy: 95%)
Test 4: Stop training works without errors - PASSED (Training stopped toast shown, no errors)
Test 5: Train button re-enabled after stopping - PASSED (button enabled after stop)
Test 6: yieldEvery: 'epoch' in tensorflowModel.js - VERIFIED (allows UI updates between epochs)
Test 7: tf.nextFrame() calls in callbacks - VERIFIED (ensures React can re-render)
Test 8: Console logs confirm callbacks firing - VERIFIED ([TF] and [UI] logs for all epochs)
critical_code_review_comments: tensorflowModel.js line 353: yieldEvery: 'epoch' correctly configured to allow UI updates
tensorflowModel.js lines 358, 363: tf.nextFrame() calls ensure React can process state updates
TrainingPanel.jsx lines 1092-1095: onEpochBegin callback correctly updates currentEpoch state
TrainingPanel.jsx lines 1096-1113: onEpochEnd callback correctly updates trainingHistory state
TrainingPanel.jsx lines 1158-1166: handleStopTraining correctly sets stopTrainingRef and re-enables training
context_for_next_testing_agent: Training UI fix verified working. The yieldEvery: 'epoch' and tf.nextFrame() calls in tensorflowModel.js allow React to update UI during TensorFlow.js training. Epoch counter increments correctly (1-10), graph appears during training showing loss/accuracy curves, status changes from training to complete. Stop training functionality works correctly - training stops, no errors occur, and Train button is re-enabled. Console logs show [TF] and [UI] prefixed messages for debugging. Note: WebGL is unavailable in headless browser but TensorFlow.js falls back to CPU backend which works correctly for training.
